steps.action: Navigate to Okta Admin Console > Reports > System Log
steps.security_note: Use a Read-Only Admin role for evidence collection, not Super Admin
steps.audit_note: System Log entries are immutable and timestamped by Okta

steps.action: Set the date range filter to cover the collection period (e.g., last 7 days)
steps.audit_note: Ensure no gaps between collection periods

steps.action: Filter by event type: user.authentication.auth_via_mfa for MFA evidence, or user.session.start for login evidence
Click Download CSV to export the filtered log data
steps.audit_note: CSV export includes all visible columns; save with date-stamped filename

steps.action: Upload the exported CSV to the evidence repository with evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Verify the export does not contain API tokens or secrets before uploading

steps.action: Navigate to Okta Admin Console > Reports > Reports
Select the report type (e.g., 'Suspicious Activity', 'Password Health') and configure the schedule
steps.audit_note: Scheduled reports run automatically and are emailed to configured recipients

steps.action: Set the delivery schedule to match your evidence collection frequency (e.g., weekly on Monday)
steps.security_note: Ensure report recipients are limited to authorised personnel only

steps.action: Configure email delivery or Okta Workflows integration for automated storage
steps.audit_note: Reports include generation timestamp and are versioned by Okta

steps.action: Create a dedicated API token in Okta Admin Console > Security > API > Tokens with read-only scopes
steps.security_note: Use a service account, not personal admin credentials. Rotate token every 90 days.
steps.audit_note: Token creation logged in System Log

steps.action: Configure automated script or integration to call GET /api/v1/logs with date filters for authentication events
Schedule collection via cron job or CI/CD pipeline to run daily, exporting results to evidence repository
steps.security_note: Store API token in secrets manager, never in plaintext config files

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed collection runs
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should be logged and trigger re-collection within SLA

steps.action: Validate collected data against expected event volume — flag anomalies if daily count drops below baseline
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Okta Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Deploy Okta Event Hook or Log Streaming to push events in real-time to your SIEM or evidence platform
steps.security_note: Use TLS 1.2+ for webhook endpoints. Verify webhook signatures.

steps.action: Configure collection completeness metrics: track event volume, latency, and coverage across all Okta org units
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Set up automated quality checks — validate schema consistency, check for gaps in time series, flag duplicate events
Create dashboard showing collection health: events/day, p95 latency, coverage %, failed collections
steps.audit_note: Dashboard serves as continuous evidence of process health

steps.action: Implement automated quarterly review that generates a collection health report and flags any SLA breaches
Sign in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to CloudTrail > Event history
steps.security_note: Use an IAM role with read-only permissions, never root credentials

steps.action: Set the date range to the evidence collection period and filter by event source (e.g., iam.amazonaws.com for IAM events)
steps.audit_note: CloudTrail retains 90 days of management events by default

steps.action: Click Download events > Download as CSV to export the filtered events
For S3 data events or extended history, navigate to CloudTrail > Trails and download from the configured S3 bucket
steps.security_note: Ensure the S3 bucket has versioning and MFA Delete enabled for integrity
steps.audit_note: CloudTrail logs in S3 are signed with digest files for tamper detection

steps.action: Save the export with a descriptive filename including the date range and upload to the evidence repository
steps.audit_note: Reference the Trail ARN in your evidence notes for traceability

steps.action: Create an organisation-level CloudTrail trail that logs all management events across all regions
Enable CloudTrail log file integrity validation to detect tampering
steps.audit_note: Digest files are generated hourly and stored in the same S3 bucket
steps.security_note: Enable MFA Delete on the S3 bucket for additional protection

steps.action: Configure CloudTrail to send events to CloudWatch Logs for real-time monitoring
steps.audit_note: CloudWatch Logs provide searchable, indexed access to trail events

steps.action: Set up CloudWatch metric filters and alarms for key events (e.g., root login, IAM policy changes)
Enable CloudTrail Organization Trail across all AWS accounts with S3 delivery and CloudWatch Logs integration
steps.security_note: Enable log file validation and encrypt trail with KMS CMK

steps.action: Create AWS Lambda function triggered by S3 PutObject events to extract and transform trail logs into evidence format
Configure AWS Config Rules to continuously evaluate compliance state — rules serve as automated evidence
Schedule daily AWS Config compliance snapshots delivered to S3 evidence bucket
steps.audit_note: Config snapshots provide point-in-time compliance evidence

steps.action: Set up CloudWatch Alarms for trail delivery failures and Config rule non-compliance events

L4.title: AWS Managed Evidence with Compliance Analytics

steps.action: Deploy AWS Security Hub to aggregate findings from Config, CloudTrail, GuardDuty, and Inspector into unified compliance view
Configure Security Hub compliance standards (CIS, PCI DSS, AWS Foundational) as continuous evidence of control effectiveness
steps.audit_note: Security Hub scores provide quantitative compliance evidence

steps.action: Create Amazon Athena queries against CloudTrail S3 logs for trend analysis and anomaly detection
Build QuickSight dashboard tracking compliance score trends, evidence collection coverage, and remediation velocity
Implement automated monthly evidence package generation — zip of Config snapshots, Security Hub reports, and trail summaries
Navigate to AWS Config > Rules in the AWS Console
Review the Compliance dashboard for current compliance status across all rules
steps.audit_note: Config evaluations are timestamped and retained per retention settings

steps.action: Select individual rules to view non-compliant resources and export the results
Take screenshots or export the compliance summary and upload as evidence
steps.audit_note: Include the Config rule names and evaluation timestamps

steps.action: Navigate to AWS Config > Rules and enable managed rules for your compliance requirements (e.g., s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited, iam-password-policy)
Configure an AWS Config Aggregator if you have multiple accounts/regions to consolidate compliance data
steps.audit_note: Aggregators provide a single pane of compliance across all accounts

steps.action: Set up an SNS topic for Config rule compliance change notifications
steps.security_note: Restrict SNS topic access to authorised subscribers only

steps.action: Create a Config Conformance Pack for your compliance framework to automate multi-rule evaluation
steps.audit_note: Conformance Packs provide framework-level compliance scoring

steps.action: Enable Config delivery channel to S3 for automated evidence archival with lifecycle policies
Deploy AWS Config Conformance Packs aligned to your compliance framework (CIS, NIST, SOC 2)
Enable Config Aggregator for multi-account, multi-region visibility
steps.security_note: Ensure aggregator has cross-account IAM roles with least privilege

steps.action: Configure automated remediation via SSM Automation documents for critical non-compliant resources
Schedule weekly Config compliance snapshots as evidence artifacts
steps.audit_note: Snapshots provide immutable point-in-time compliance records

L4.title: AWS Config Managed Compliance with Drift Detection

steps.action: Implement Config rule coverage metrics — track percentage of resources under evaluation vs total resources
Create drift detection pipeline: compare current Config state against approved baseline configurations
Build compliance trend dashboard showing compliant/non-compliant resource counts over time
steps.audit_note: Trend data demonstrates sustained compliance, not just point-in-time

steps.action: Automate evidence report generation with compliance percentages, remediation times, and drift incidents
Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center (entra.microsoft.com) > Identity > Monitoring > Sign-in logs
steps.security_note: Use a least-privilege role; Global Reader works but is broader than needed

steps.action: Set the date range filter to the evidence collection period
steps.audit_note: Entra ID retains sign-in logs for 30 days (free) or 30 days+ (P1/P2 license)

steps.action: Apply filters: Status = Success/Failure, Conditional Access = desired policy, or MFA requirement as needed
Click Download > CSV to export the filtered sign-in data
steps.audit_note: CSV includes correlation ID for cross-referencing with other logs

steps.action: For audit logs, navigate to Identity > Monitoring > Audit logs and repeat the export process
Upload both exports to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Review exports for any PII that should be redacted before sharing

L2.title: Entra ID Diagnostic Settings Automated Collection

steps.action: Navigate to Entra admin center > Identity > Monitoring > Diagnostic settings > Add diagnostic setting
Select the log categories to stream: SignInLogs, AuditLogs, NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, ManagedIdentitySignInLogs
steps.audit_note: Streaming to Log Analytics extends retention beyond the default 30 days

steps.action: Configure destination: Send to Log Analytics workspace for querying, or Archive to storage account for long-term retention
steps.security_note: Ensure the storage account has encryption at rest and restricted access

steps.action: Create saved KQL queries in Log Analytics for your evidence types (e.g., MFA usage, conditional access outcomes)
Set up scheduled query alerts in Azure Monitor for compliance-critical events
steps.audit_note: Scheduled queries can export results to storage for automated evidence archival

steps.action: Configure Entra ID Diagnostic Settings to stream Sign-in Logs and Audit Logs to Log Analytics Workspace
Create KQL queries in Log Analytics to extract evidence-specific data — MFA usage, conditional access hits, risky sign-ins
Schedule Logic App or Azure Function to run daily KQL queries and export results to evidence repository
Configure Azure Monitor alerts for collection failures and anomalous event volumes
steps.audit_note: Alerting on collection failures demonstrates process maturity

steps.action: Set up automated weekly identity governance reports — access reviews, privilege changes, guest account activity

L4.title: Entra ID Managed Identity Governance Pipeline

steps.action: Deploy Microsoft Entra ID Governance with automated access reviews on a recurring schedule
Configure Privileged Identity Management (PIM) with just-in-time activation metrics tracking
steps.audit_note: PIM activation logs serve as evidence of least-privilege enforcement

steps.action: Build identity evidence dashboard: access review completion rates, PIM activation frequency, conditional access coverage
Implement automated quarterly identity attestation report generation with trend analysis
Navigate to your GitHub Organization > Settings > Audit log
steps.security_note: Audit log access requires Owner role; consider creating a dedicated audit account

steps.action: Use the search bar to filter events (e.g., action:repo.create for new repos, action:org.update_member for membership changes)
steps.audit_note: GitHub audit log retains events for 180 days (Enterprise Cloud) or 90 days (free)

steps.action: Click Export > JSON or CSV to download the filtered audit log entries
For branch protection evidence, navigate to repo > Settings > Branches and screenshot the protection rules
steps.audit_note: Branch protection changes appear in the audit log with before/after values

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Ensure no personal access tokens or webhook secrets are visible in screenshots

L2.title: GitHub Audit Log Streaming & API Automation

steps.action: Navigate to Organization Settings > Audit log > Audit log streaming and configure a stream destination (S3, Azure Blob, Datadog, or Splunk)
steps.security_note: Audit log streaming requires Enterprise Cloud plan

steps.action: Use the GitHub REST API to programmatically query branch protection rules: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/{branch}/protection
Create a scheduled GitHub Action workflow that exports audit log data using the Audit Log API and stores it as a workflow artifact
steps.audit_note: GitHub Actions workflows are themselves audited in the audit log

steps.action: Configure Dependabot alerts and security advisories to auto-create issues for vulnerability tracking
Enable GitHub Audit Log Streaming to your SIEM or S3 bucket via enterprise settings
steps.security_note: Use dedicated service credentials for the streaming endpoint

steps.action: Create GitHub Actions workflow that calls the Audit Log API daily to export organisation-level events
steps.permissions_required: Organization admin PAT with admin:org scope

steps.action: Automate branch protection compliance checks — script that verifies all repos meet required protection rules via API
Configure Dependabot and code scanning alerts as automated security evidence
steps.audit_note: Automated scanning results serve as evidence of secure development practices

steps.action: Set up alerting for audit log streaming failures and policy violations
Deploy GitHub Advanced Security metrics tracking — code scanning alerts, secret scanning, dependency review across all repos
Create evidence dashboard: MTTR for security alerts, code review coverage %, branch protection compliance rate
Implement automated compliance gating in CI/CD — PRs cannot merge without passing security checks, serving as continuous evidence
Build monthly DevSecOps evidence package: scanning trends, remediation velocity, policy compliance rates
steps.audit_note: Trend data demonstrates sustained security practice maturity

steps.action: Configure automated quarterly review of GitHub organisation settings, SSO enforcement, and IP allow lists
Navigate to Jira and open the relevant project board (e.g., Change Management, IT Operations)
Create a JQL filter for change management evidence: project = 'CM' AND created >= -7d ORDER BY created DESC
steps.audit_note: Save this filter for consistent weekly collections; JQL filters are auditable

steps.action: Click the Export icon (top-right) and select 'Export Excel CSV (all fields)' to download the filtered results
For approval evidence, open individual tickets and screenshot the workflow transitions showing approver names and timestamps
steps.audit_note: Jira workflow transitions include user and timestamp metadata

steps.action: Upload the CSV export and any approval screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
Save a JQL filter for change management evidence: project = CHANGE AND status changed DURING (startOfWeek(), endOfWeek())
Configure Jira Automation rule to export the saved filter results as CSV weekly and email to evidence mailbox
Set up a Confluence page that auto-embeds the JQL results as a living change log
steps.audit_note: Confluence page provides persistent, auditable evidence trail

steps.action: Create Jira API token and configure automated script to query /rest/api/3/search with JQL for change/incident tickets
steps.permissions_required: API token with Browse Projects permission

steps.action: Automate daily extraction of change records with approval chains, implementation dates, and rollback status
Implement data validation — verify all changes have required fields: approver, change type, risk rating
Configure webhook for real-time change tracking to evidence platform
steps.audit_note: Real-time tracking eliminates gaps between change execution and evidence capture

steps.action: Build change management metrics dashboard: change frequency, approval cycle time, emergency change ratio, rollback rate
Implement automated CAB (Change Advisory Board) compliance checking — flag changes missing required approvals
Create trend analysis: compare change success rates month-over-month, identify problematic change categories
Generate automated monthly change management evidence report with KPIs and trend charts
Navigate to ServiceNow and open the Change Management module (Change > All)
Set the list filter to show changes within the evidence period: Created on >= [start date] AND Created on <= [end date]
steps.audit_note: ServiceNow change records include full audit trail of field changes

steps.action: Right-click the column header and select 'Export > CSV' to download the filtered change list
steps.permissions_required: ITIL role with list export permissions

steps.action: For incident evidence, navigate to Incident > All and repeat with appropriate filters (e.g., Priority = 1 or 2)
steps.audit_note: Include incident SLA data for response time evidence

steps.action: Upload the exports to the evidence repository with descriptive filenames and evidence ID references
Create a ServiceNow Scheduled Report for change_request and incident tables with date filters for the collection period
Configure report delivery to evidence repository email or shared drive on a weekly schedule
Set up a separate scheduled report for SLA achievement metrics as evidence of process maturity
steps.audit_note: SLA reports provide quantitative evidence of service management effectiveness

steps.action: Create a ServiceNow integration user and OAuth application for API access
steps.security_note: Use OAuth 2.0 with client credentials grant, not basic auth

steps.action: Configure automated script to query Table API for change_request, incident, and problem records
Implement automated CMDB accuracy validation — compare CMDB records against actual infrastructure state
Set up IntegrationHub flow to push evidence artifacts to external evidence platform on record closure

L4.title: ServiceNow Managed ITSM Evidence with Analytics

steps.action: Deploy Performance Analytics dashboards tracking ITSM KPIs: MTTR, change success rate, SLA compliance, first-contact resolution
Configure automated compliance scoring using ServiceNow GRC module or custom scorecards
Implement continuous monitoring rules that flag ITSM process deviations in real-time
Generate automated monthly ITSM evidence package: KPI trends, SLA reports, process compliance scores
Sign in to Splunk Web and navigate to Search & Reporting
steps.permissions_required: power or user role with access to relevant indices

steps.action: Enter an SPL query for your evidence type (e.g., index=main sourcetype=syslog | stats count by host for system inventory, or index=security action=success | stats count by user for authentication evidence)
steps.audit_note: Document the exact SPL query used for reproducibility and audit trail
steps.security_note: Ensure your search does not return sensitive fields like passwords or API keys

steps.action: Set the time range picker to the evidence collection period
steps.audit_note: Splunk timestamps are derived from event data and indexed at ingestion time

steps.action: Click Export and select CSV format to download the search results
Optionally, save the search as a Report for future scheduled execution: Save As > Report
Upload the CSV export to the evidence repository with evidence ID reference
Convert your SPL evidence queries into Saved Searches with weekly schedule
Configure Saved Search actions to export results as CSV to a monitored evidence directory or send via email
Create a Splunk Dashboard with all evidence-related searches for visual verification
steps.audit_note: Dashboard provides at-a-glance evidence of log monitoring activity

steps.action: Create Splunk REST API integration to programmatically run evidence collection searches
Deploy Splunk Summary Indexing to pre-compute evidence metrics for fast retrieval
Implement collection completeness monitoring — track data sources, index volumes, and ingestion latency
Configure Splunk alerts for evidence collection failures: missing sourcetypes, volume drops, parsing errors
steps.audit_note: Alerting on collection health demonstrates mature monitoring practice

L4.title: Splunk Managed Evidence with Compliance Analytics

steps.action: Deploy Splunk Enterprise Security (ES) or InfoSec app as structured compliance evidence framework
Configure ES compliance dashboards tracking detection coverage, alert fidelity, and investigation times
Implement automated evidence quality scoring: completeness, timeliness, accuracy metrics per evidence type
Build automated monthly security monitoring evidence package: notable events, investigation outcomes, compliance posture trends
Sign in to the CrowdStrike Falcon console (falcon.crowdstrike.com)
steps.security_note: Use the minimum privilege role; avoid using Falcon Administrator for read-only tasks

steps.action: Navigate to Activity > Detections and set the time filter to the evidence collection period
steps.audit_note: Falcon detection events include kill chain stage, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, and severity

steps.action: Apply filters for severity level and detection status as needed, then click Export to download as CSV
Navigate to Host Management > Sensor Coverage to verify endpoint protection coverage
steps.audit_note: Sensor coverage percentage is key evidence for endpoint protection controls

steps.action: Export the sensor coverage report and dashboard screenshot for coverage evidence
Upload all exports to the evidence repository with evidence ID references
Configure Falcon Scheduled Reports for detections, sensor coverage, and prevention policy compliance
Set up email delivery of scheduled reports to evidence collection mailbox on weekly cadence
Create a Falcon custom dashboard summarising endpoint protection posture as visual evidence
steps.audit_note: Dashboard screenshots serve as evidence of continuous endpoint monitoring

steps.action: Create CrowdStrike API client with Detections Read, Hosts Read, and Prevention Policies Read scopes
steps.security_note: Store client ID and secret in a secrets manager. Rotate annually.

steps.action: Implement automated daily collection: GET /detects/queries/detects/v1 for detections, /devices/queries/devices/v1 for coverage
Configure SIEM integration via Falcon Data Replicator (FDR) or Falcon LogScale for real-time event streaming
Set up automated sensor coverage gap detection — flag endpoints missing Falcon agent
steps.audit_note: Coverage gap reporting demonstrates proactive security management

steps.action: Deploy Falcon Spotlight vulnerability metrics alongside detection data for holistic endpoint evidence
Build endpoint evidence dashboard: detection-to-resolution MTTR, sensor coverage %, prevention policy compliance, vulnerability remediation rate
Implement automated weekly evidence quality validation — verify all expected data sources are reporting within SLA
Generate automated monthly endpoint protection evidence package: detection trends, coverage metrics, policy compliance, vulnerability posture
Sign in to your cloud provider's management console with a read-only role
steps.security_note: Never use root/admin credentials for routine evidence collection

steps.action: Navigate to the audit log or activity log section of the console
steps.audit_note: Most cloud providers retain activity logs for 90 days by default

steps.action: Filter the log by date range, event type, and any relevant resource filters
steps.audit_note: Document your filter criteria for reproducibility

steps.action: Export the filtered results as CSV or JSON
steps.security_note: Review exports for sensitive data before storing

steps.action: Upload to the evidence repository with a descriptive filename and evidence ID reference
Configure scheduled exports from cloud provider's native reporting tools (activity logs, compliance reports)
Set up automated delivery to evidence repository via email or storage integration
Create compliance dashboard in cloud provider's native console for visual evidence
Create service account or API credentials with read-only access to audit and compliance APIs
Implement automated daily collection scripts calling provider APIs for logs, configuration state, and compliance data
Configure alerting for collection failures and data volume anomalies
Validate collected evidence against expected coverage — all accounts, all regions, all resource types
Deploy cloud-native compliance framework (e.g., Security Hub, Defender for Cloud, Security Command Center)
Build evidence dashboard tracking compliance scores, remediation velocity, and coverage metrics over time
Implement automated evidence package generation with trend analysis and KPI reporting
Sign in to your identity provider's admin console with a read-only admin role
steps.security_note: Use the minimum privilege role that can access reports

steps.action: Navigate to Reports or Logs section and select authentication/sign-in logs
Set the date range to cover the evidence collection period with no gaps from the last collection
steps.audit_note: Check your provider's log retention period to avoid data loss

steps.action: Export the filtered log data as CSV and upload to the evidence repository
Configure scheduled reports for authentication events, access changes, and policy violations
Set up automated report delivery to evidence collection mailbox or shared storage
Create identity dashboard showing authentication patterns and policy compliance
Create API credentials with read-only access to identity provider's log and audit APIs
Automate daily extraction of authentication events, access reviews, and privilege changes
Implement volume and completeness validation for collected identity evidence
Configure alerting for collection failures and suspicious identity events
Deploy identity governance automation — scheduled access reviews, privilege attestation, dormant account detection
Build identity evidence dashboard: MFA adoption, access review completion rates, privilege escalation trends
Generate automated monthly identity governance evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your ticketing system and navigate to the relevant project or queue
Create a filter or search query for the evidence period (e.g., change requests created in the last 7 days)
steps.audit_note: Save your filter for repeatable future collections

steps.action: Export the filtered results as CSV including ticket ID, status, approvals, and dates
Upload the export to the evidence repository with evidence ID reference
Create saved filters for compliance-relevant ticket types (changes, incidents, requests)
Configure scheduled export of filtered results as CSV or PDF reports
Set up automated delivery to evidence repository
Create API integration to query ticketing system for change, incident, and problem records
Automate daily extraction with data validation — verify required fields are populated
Implement webhook integration for real-time evidence capture on ticket closure
Build ITSM metrics dashboard: change success rate, MTTR, SLA compliance, approval cycle time
Implement automated compliance scoring for ITSM processes
Generate automated monthly ITSM evidence package with KPI trends
Sign in to your SIEM or log management platform
Create a search query for the evidence type you need (e.g., authentication events, configuration changes)
steps.audit_note: Document your search query for reproducibility and audit trail

steps.action: Set the time range to the evidence collection period and execute the search
Export results as CSV or JSON and upload to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Check that exported logs do not contain passwords or tokens in cleartext

steps.action: Create saved searches for evidence-relevant log queries and schedule weekly execution
Configure automated export of search results to evidence repository
Set up logging dashboard for visual evidence of monitoring activity
Create API integration for programmatic log search and export
Implement automated collection completeness monitoring — track data sources and ingestion health
Configure alerting for log collection failures and volume anomalies
Deploy security analytics framework for structured compliance evidence
Build evidence quality dashboard: completeness, timeliness, accuracy metrics
Generate automated monthly log monitoring evidence package with trend analysis
Sign in to your security tool's management console
Navigate to the Reports or Detections section relevant to your evidence need
Set date range filters and any relevant severity or category filters
steps.audit_note: Include the tool version and scan coverage in your evidence notes

steps.action: Export the report as PDF or CSV and upload to the evidence repository
Configure scheduled reports for security findings, coverage metrics, and policy compliance
Set up automated report delivery to evidence repository
Create security posture dashboard as visual evidence
Create API credentials for automated access to security tool's detection and coverage APIs
Implement daily automated collection of findings, coverage data, and policy compliance state
Configure SIEM integration for real-time security event streaming
Set up alerting for collection failures and coverage gaps
Build security evidence dashboard: MTTR, coverage %, detection fidelity, policy compliance trends
Implement automated evidence quality validation and completeness scoring
Generate automated monthly security evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your code repository platform and navigate to organization/project settings
Access the audit log or activity stream for the organization
steps.permissions_required: Organization admin or audit log viewer

steps.action: Filter events by date range and event type (e.g., repository changes, access changes, branch protection)
steps.audit_note: Note the log retention period for your plan tier

steps.action: Export filtered events and upload to the evidence repository with evidence ID reference
Configure scheduled exports of audit logs, access reviews, and security scanning results
Set up automated delivery of branch protection compliance reports
Create development security dashboard as visual evidence
Create API integration for automated audit log and security scanning result collection
Automate branch protection compliance verification across all repositories
Configure CI/CD pipeline evidence capture — build logs, security scan results, deployment records
Build DevSecOps evidence dashboard: code review coverage, security alert MTTR, policy compliance rate
Implement automated compliance gating in CI/CD as continuous evidence
Generate automated monthly DevSecOps evidence package with security metrics and trends
Access your document management system and locate the relevant policy or procedure documents
Verify the document version, approval date, and next review date
steps.audit_note: Document version history provides evidence of regular review cycles

steps.action: Export the document as PDF (including metadata) and upload to the evidence repository
If available, export the version history or approval workflow as additional evidence
Configure scheduled reports for document access logs, sharing changes, and DLP policy events
Set up automated delivery of audit reports to evidence repository
Create document governance dashboard as visual evidence
Create API integration for automated access log and sharing audit collection
Automate DLP policy compliance evidence extraction
Configure alerting for sensitive document access anomalies
